untranslatable_reason: This signal requires real-time option pricing data (call moneyness, intrinsic value, parity premium, interest rates on short sale, dividend forecasting) which are not available in the standard price/technical indicator set. The strategy is a complex options arbitrage that cannot be expressed using only OHLCV and technical indicators.
translation_notes: MCMILLAN_DRY_192 describes an 'Interest Play' — a synthetic arbitrage combining a short stock position with a long in-the-money call, where profits depend on the call trading slightly over parity and the premium being justified by short-sale interest and dividends. This requires: (1) option chain data with strike prices and premiums, (2) real-time dividend calendar, (3) short rebate rates. A backtest engine using only price/volume/technical indicators cannot model option pricing dynamics or the cost-of-carry arbitrage relationships required for this strategy. This signal is untranslatable to a pure technical rule set.
